"Alcohol isn’t a social lubricant—it’s a corporate pacifier.<[silence:400]> The myth? That booze ‘loosens you up’ for connection.<[silence:400]> The truth? It’s a $1.6T industry engineered to hijack your GABA receptors, turning anxiety into dependency while your prefrontal cortex—the part that questions why you’re drinking—goes dark (NIAAA, 2023).<[silence:400]> That ‘fun’ buzz? A chemical lobotomy:<[silence:100]> ethanol disrupts memory formation so badly that blackouts aren’t failures of recall—they’re erased time (White et al., Alcoholism:<[silence:100]> Clinical & Experimental Research, 2004).<[silence:400]> Meanwhile, the ‘moderation’ lie crumbles under math:<[silence:100]> Just 1 drink daily spikes breast cancer risk by 9% (JAMA Network, 2020), and the WHO admits no safe level exists for carcinogens.<[silence:400]> Your ‘harmless habit’? A slow-motion bet against your own brain, liver, and DNA—with the house (Big Alcohol) always winning.<[silence:400]>

Still call it ‘relaxing’ when the withdrawal tremors start?"

clean_story:
"Alcohol isn’t a social lubricant—it’s a corporate pacifier. The myth? That booze ‘loosens you up’ for connection. The truth? It’s a $1.6T industry engineered to hijack your GABA receptors, turning anxiety into dependency while your prefrontal cortex—the part that questions why you’re drinking—goes dark (NIAAA, 2023). That ‘fun’ buzz? A chemical lobotomy: ethanol disrupts memory formation so badly that blackouts aren’t failures of recall—they’re erased time (White et al., Alcoholism: Clinical & Experimental Research, 2004). Meanwhile, the ‘moderation’ lie crumbles under math: Just 1 drink daily spikes breast cancer risk by 9% (JAMA Network, 2020), and the WHO admits no safe level exists for carcinogens. Your ‘harmless habit’? A slow-motion bet against your own brain, liver, and DNA—with the house (Big Alcohol) always winning.

Still call it ‘relaxing’ when the withdrawal tremors start?"

(599 chars)
